data.narrative:
Second Front Systems is a Series B-stage defense and security company founded by Peter Dixon, Mark Butler, and Nate Hughes, having raised $90M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 peers—2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms operating in the same sector and stage, including Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses the $90M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implying a $171M valuation. The peer-median method examines 7 same-sector, same-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $300M to $3.0B; the cohort median stands at $1.0B, reflecting the diversity within frontier defense tech.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $145M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $586M. The substantial spread reflects meaningful uncertainty around market positioning relative to peers. Stage-progression conservatively anchors the lower bound, while peer comparables—particularly Dream Security at $3.0B—establish the upper boundary. Key uncertainties include Second Front's revenue scale and growth trajectory relative to the named peer set, neither of which are present in available data. The $586M median represents the midpoint of observable methodologies and should be stress-tested against operational metrics before final recommendation.
